source_pr.title: executor: fix unstable TestMaxPreparedStmtCount
source_pr.body:
<!--

Thank you for contributing to TiDB!

PR Title Format:
1. pkg [, pkg2, pkg3]: what's changed
2. *: what's changed

-->

### What problem does this PR solve?
<!--

Please create an issue first to describe the problem.

There MUST be one line starting with "Issue Number:  " and 
linking the relevant issues via the "close" or "ref".

For more info, check https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/contribute-code.html#referring-to-an-issue.

-->

Issue Number: ref #39735

Problem Summary:

### What is changed and how it works?

Set `variable.PreparedStmtCount` to 0 when running `TestMaxPreparedStmtCount`. In this way, prepared statements executed in other testcases would not disturb the testcase.

### Check List

Tests <!-- At least one of them must be included. -->

- [ ] Unit test
- [ ] Integration test
- [ ] Manual test (add detailed scripts or steps below)
- [ ] No code

Side effects

- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more CPU
- [ ] Performance regression: Consumes more Memory
- [ ] Breaking backward compatibility

Documentation

- [ ] Affects user behaviors
- [ ] Contains syntax changes
- [ ] Contains variable changes
- [ ] Contains experimental features
- [ ] Changes MySQL compatibility

### Release note

<!-- compatibility change, improvement, bugfix, and new feature need a release note -->

Please refer to [Release Notes Language Style Guide](https://pingcap.github.io/tidb-dev-guide/contribute-to-tidb/release-notes-style-guide.html) to write a quality release note.

```release-note
None
```

symptoms: `TestMaxPreparedStmtCount` is unstable because a global counter (`variable.PreparedStmtCount`) can be left non-zero by other tests.
failure_signature: `TestMaxPreparedStmtCount` fails intermittently depending on previous tests that incremented `PreparedStmtCount` (suite-order dependency).
root_cause_explanation: `TestMaxPreparedStmtCount` relies on the global counter `variable.PreparedStmtCount` starting from zero. When other tests run before it, the counter may already be incremented, making the test order-dependent and flaky. The fix saves the current value, resets `PreparedStmtCount` to 0 at the beginning of the test, and restores it via `defer`, isolating the test from suite-global state.
fix_pattern: Save/restore global counters used by tests (e.g. `PreparedStmtCount`): reset to a known value at test start and restore in `defer` to avoid suite-order dependency.
